name: eval
label: eval
description: Run code snippets in a configured runtime using a temporary file. Available languages: {{availableLanguages}}. Default timeout: {{defaultTimeoutMs}}ms.{{maxTimeoutDescription}}
promptSnippet: Run Node.js or Python snippets without shell quoting
promptGuidelines: Use eval for language-specific experiments when a shell pipeline is not needed.
The language argument must be one of the available configured languages.
Each call runs in a fresh subprocess from a temporary file; there is no persistent REPL/session state.
Temporary code files are removed after execution.
Use bash instead for package-manager commands, project scripts, git, and shell pipelines.

language.description: Runtime to execute the code with. Available configured languages: {{availableLanguages}}.

code.description: Code to write to a temporary file and execute. The temp file is removed after execution.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds. If omitted, defaults to {{defaultTimeoutMs}}ms.{{maxTimeoutDescription}}

cwd.description: Working directory for the subprocess. Relative paths are resolved from the current workspace. If omitted, uses the current workspace.
